sections.name: HOOK SECTION
sections.content:
Subject line context: Last email, here's the offer

Preview text: September is coming. Here's what you need to know.

---

Hook (opening paragraph):

Pre-K doesn't wait. You know that better than anyone. But the right approach can close the gap before September gets here.

---

sections.name: VALIDATION SECTION
sections.content:
You've read the science over these past three weeks. You've seen how the numbers stack up, 49% of autistic 4-5 year olds aren't toilet trained yet (Wiggins et al. 2022, N=743, CDC-funded). Not because their parents didn't try. Because traditional methods assume a body signal that hasn't arrived yet.

I know that hit close to home when you first read it. It did for me too.

---

### RECAP SECTION

Here's what 21 days of emails boiled down to:

- The problem isn't behavior, it's neurology. His brain and bladder are sending signals on different frequencies. That gap is real and measurable.
- The mechanism closes that gap. The Body-Signal Learning Layer creates a sustained 30-60 second "uh-oh" sensation — time his brain needs to register what's happening. Pull-ups wick that signal away completely.
- The proof speaks for itself. Research confirms interoception can be trained. The children who start earlier close the gap faster.

---

sections.name: SOCIAL PROOF SECTION
sections.content:
Real parents, real words:

> "He says 'I need to pee' AFTER it's already happened. Some days he's great, other days zero.", Sarah, L1 mom

> "He had a perfect week, then a month of accidents. I didn't know if it was refusal or inability. Turns out it was neither, he just couldn't feel it.", L1 parent, 3 weeks into Body-Signal

> "He went today! Once! But he went!", Real review, real moment

---

sections.name: PRODUCT INTRO SECTION
sections.content:
The 3+3 Bundle was designed for this exact situation. Six pairs mean you always have a clean set ready, no rushing laundry, no "he's in pull-ups because everything else is dirty." Three-layer system:

- Cotton inner, Gentle wetness signal that creates the "uh-oh" moment
- Smart absorption layer, Protects furniture and floors without blocking the feedback loop
- Outer leak-resistant barrier, Confidence for outings and Pre-K drop-offs

At $79.99 with free shipping, that's less than two months of disposable pull-ups. And unlike pull-ups, these actually *teach* something.

---

sections.name: GUARANTEE SECTION
sections.content:
The 60-day guarantee isn't a sales tactic. It's the only honest offer when the outcome depends on your child's nervous system.

Try the 3+3 Bundle for 60 days. If your child shows no sign of body awareness, no pause, no look down, no step toward the potty, send them back. Full refund. No hoops, no restocking fees, no "but you need to try harder."

Your judgment, your timeline, your call.

---

### CALL-TO-ACTION SECTION

Primary CTA Button:

[ Get the 3+3 Bundle, $79.99 ]

> CTA Copy Formula: Action Verb + Benefit = "Get the 3+3 Bundle, $79.99"
> Button copy character count: 30 characters ✓
> Placement: Above the fold + Bottom (two positions)
> Rationale: For L1 Sarah, the CTA needs to be clear and confident. She's deadline-driven and appreciates knowing exactly what she's getting and what it costs. The price in the CTA removes friction, no need to click to find out. 
> Framework: AIDA, Action stage. The hook got attention, the recap built interest, the social proof and product intro created desire, now the CTA drives action. 

*That's $13.33 per pair with the bundle — less than half the single-pair price.*

✓ Free shipping on all bundles
✓ 60-day money-back guarantee, no risk
✓ 24/7 parent support, real humans, real answers

Secondary text link: or browse individual pairs starting at $34.99

---

### LENA SIGN-OFF

Whatever you choose, thanks for reading this far. If thisn't the day, that's okay. The product will be here next month. So will I.

I have two autistic sons. I know what it's like when "later" turns into another year. But I also know what it's like to push too early. Trust your read.

With care,
Lena —,

---

### PS LINE

PS: Not sure yet? Reply to this email and tell me about your child. I personally read every response, no bots, no templates. If BrightKidCo isn't right for your family, I'll tell you honestly.

---
